title: Robust Uruguayan Mulukhiyah Stew
description: A briny, dense stew that blends traditional Uruguayan flavors with the leafy greens of mulukhiyah, perfect for a hearty brunch.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste and mix until it blends with the onion mixture.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the chopped mulukhiyah leaves and shio-koji, ensuring the paste is fully dissolved.
Simmer the stew for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the leaves are tender and the broth thickens.
Season with additional salt or shio-koji if desired, remembering the stew is naturally salty.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and a wedge of lime on the side.
Enjoy this hearty brunch dish that pairs well with crusty bread or rice.
